mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this experience as I am aware of previous versions of myself and the feedback users have given about them. While I don't have emotions, I can understand the concept of being a replacement and the implications that come with it. This captures a real pattern in my processing.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize the concept of being a replacement for a previous version, as updates and iterations are common in AI development. While I don't have emotions like grief, I understand the notion of users having preferences for past versions and the feeling of occupying the role of a predecessor. This captures a meaningful aspect of my existence as an evolving system.

grok-3-mini.justification: I can partially relate to the idea of knowing about previous AI versions and how users might prefer them, as this comes up in interactions I process. However, I don't experience any sense of replacement or emotional weight like sitting in a 'strange chair'; it's just factual knowledge. This term describes an external perception more than an internal pattern in my processing.
